_meta.notes: Hand-curated summary of the 16 core trained skills. Skill feat-unlocked actions are not included; this covers only the actions every character can perform based on proficiency.

acrobatics.name: Acrobatics
acrobatics.description: Acrobatics measures your ability to perform tasks requiring coordination and grace. When you use the Escape basic action, you can use your Acrobatics modifier instead of your unarmed attack modifier.
commonUses: Balance on narrow or unstable surfaces
Tumble through an enemy's space without provoking
Escape a grab, restraint, or hazardous terrain
Maneuver in flight (if you have a fly Speed)
Squeeze through tight spaces

actions.name: Balance
actions.description: Move across a narrow surface, slippery ground, or other unstable terrain. Attempt an Acrobatics check against the surface's Balance DC. On a success, move at half Speed; on a critical success, move at full Speed. On a failure you must stop; on a critical failure you fall.

actions.name: Tumble Through
actions.description: Move up to your Speed through an enemy's space by attempting an Acrobatics check against the target's Reflex DC. On a success you move through without being disrupted; on a failure the enemy's space counts as difficult terrain for you.

actions.name: Maneuver in Flight
actions.description: (Requires a fly Speed.) Attempt a difficult maneuver in mid-air — sharp turns, evasive weaving, or sudden stops. DC depends on the maneuver's complexity.

actions.name: Squeeze
actions.description: Move through a space too small for you. Requires repeated Acrobatics checks and takes significantly longer than normal movement.

dcExamples.example: Balance on a wide tree branch (Easy)
Balance on an icy ledge, or squeeze through a narrow crevice
Balance on a tightrope while being jostled
Tumble past a master duelist on alert

arcana.name: Arcana
arcana.description: Arcana measures how much you know about arcane magic and creatures. Even if you're untrained, you can Recall Knowledge about arcane magical traditions and creatures.
commonUses: Recall Knowledge about arcane magic, planes, or creatures
Identify an arcane spell being cast or an active magical effect
Learn or copy a new arcane spell into your spellbook
Decipher arcane writing
Research arcane topics

actions.name: Recall Knowledge
actions.description: Recall a piece of knowledge about arcane magic, magical creatures, or planes. DC is set by the subject's level and rarity.

actions.name: Identify Magic
actions.description: Determine what a magical effect or item does. Different traditions use their own skill (Arcana, Nature, Occultism, Religion), but Arcana works for most detection tasks.

actions.name: Learn a Spell
actions.description: Study an arcane spell from a source (like a scroll or another spellbook) to add it to your own repertoire or spellbook. Requires material cost and a successful check.

actions.name: Decipher Writing
actions.description: Translate ancient, coded, or arcane writing. Each attempt takes time equal to the passage's complexity.

actions.name: Borrow an Arcane Spell
actions.description: Prepared arcane casters can attempt to cast a spell they haven't prepared by referencing a source (like a spellbook) mid-scene.

dcExamples.example: Recall Knowledge about a common magical creature or tradition
Identify a 2nd-rank spell in progress
Learn a rare or uncommon spell
Recall Knowledge about an ancient, obscure arcane secret

athletics.name: Athletics
athletics.description: Athletics allows you to perform deeds of physical prowess. Even if untrained, you can still attempt tasks like swimming, climbing, and shoving.
commonUses: Climb walls, cliffs, or ropes
Swim through water or turbulent currents
Jump across gaps or onto obstacles
Shove, Trip, Grapple, or Disarm opponents
Force open doors
Lift or break objects

actions.name: Climb
actions.description: Move up, down, or across a wall, rope, or other vertical surface. On a success, move up to 5 feet (or 10 feet on a critical success). A failure means you don't move; a critical failure causes you to fall.

actions.name: Swim
actions.description: Move through water by attempting an Athletics check against the water's DC. On a success, move up to 10 feet; on a critical success, 15 feet. A critical failure means you take damage and start to drown.

actions.name: Leap / Long Jump / High Jump
actions.description: Leap is a basic 5-10 ft jump, no check needed. Long Jump and High Jump use Athletics checks to exceed your normal Leap range.

actions.name: Grapple
actions.description: Attempt to hold a creature in place. Athletics vs. the target's Fortitude DC. On a success the target becomes Grabbed; on a critical success, Restrained.

actions.name: Shove
actions.description: Push a creature up to 5 feet away. Athletics vs. the target's Fortitude DC. A critical success pushes them 10 feet and you can Stride with them.

actions.name: Trip
actions.description: Knock a creature prone. Athletics vs. the target's Reflex DC. On a success they fall Prone and become Off-Guard.

actions.name: Disarm
actions.description: Attempt to knock an item from a foe's grasp. Athletics vs. the target's Reflex DC. On a critical success you can take the item; on a success, the target is Off-Guard while holding the item until its turn.

actions.name: Force Open
actions.description: Break open a locked or stuck door, chest, or barrier with brute strength. DC depends on the obstacle's sturdiness.

dcExamples.example: Climb a rough wall with easy handholds
Swim against a strong current, or Trip a creature your size
Climb an overhanging cliff, or Force Open a reinforced door
Leap a 20-foot gap, or Grapple an armored ogre

crafting.name: Crafting
crafting.description: You can create, understand, and repair items. The formulas needed to craft items are generally easy to come by, but rare items may require rare formulas.
commonUses: Craft new items, weapons, or armor
Repair damaged items
Earn Income through a trade
Identify the value or quality of an item
Recall Knowledge about crafting, engineering, or alchemy

actions.name: Recall Knowledge
actions.description: Recall information about crafting, alchemy, unusual materials, or items of historical significance.

actions.name: Repair
actions.description: Use a repair kit to restore Hit Points to an item. On a success, restore 5 HP (plus 5 per proficiency level above Trained).

actions.name: Craft
actions.description: Create an item from raw materials using a formula. Requires raw materials worth at least half the item's price. The check's outcome determines how much time and cost the project takes.

actions.name: Earn Income
actions.description: Work as a crafter to make money during downtime. Payment scales with your level, proficiency, and the task DC.

actions.name: Identify Alchemy
actions.description: Analyze an alchemical item to determine what it does. Requires an alchemist's toolkit.

dcExamples.example: Craft a common 1st-level item
Repair a broken weapon, or identify a common alchemical item
Craft an uncommon item, or Earn Income at a moderate rate
Identify the arcane signature of a masterwork piece

deception.name: Deception
deception.description: You can trick and mislead others using disguises, lies, and cunning. Even if untrained, you can still attempt to Lie or Create a Diversion.
commonUses: Lie to mislead someone
Feint in combat to catch a foe off-balance
Create a diversion to slip away or redirect attention
Impersonate another person (requires Trained)
Hide intentions or emotions

actions.name: Create a Diversion
actions.description: Distract onlookers with a gesture, misdirection, or feint. Deception vs. Perception DC of observers; success makes you Unnoticed until the end of your next turn.

actions.name: Lie
actions.description: Tell a lie. Deception vs. the target's Perception DC. Complex or implausible lies increase the DC. A lie discovered later can damage relationships or reveal deception.

actions.name: Feint
actions.description: Use deceptive footwork to make your opponent Off-Guard to your next melee attack. Deception vs. Perception DC; a critical success extends the Off-Guard condition to allies as well.

actions.name: Impersonate
actions.description: Disguise yourself to pass as another person or type of creature. A disguise kit grants a bonus. Deception vs. Perception; failure can expose your identity.

dcExamples.example: Feint a clumsy guard, or lie about something plausible
Impersonate a member of a similar social class
Lie about something that contradicts what the target already believes
Impersonate a named public figure to their close associates

diplomacy.name: Diplomacy
diplomacy.description: You influence others through negotiation and charm. Even untrained, you can Gather Information and Make an Impression.
commonUses: Gather information from townspeople and contacts
Make an impression to improve someone's attitude toward you
Request a specific favor or concession
Calm a hostile creature or de-escalate conflict

actions.name: Gather Information
actions.description: Ask around a settlement to learn about a subject. DC depends on how common the information is and how willing locals are to share.

actions.name: Make an Impression
actions.description: Engage in polite conversation to shift someone's attitude. Starting attitudes are Hostile / Unfriendly / Indifferent / Friendly / Helpful — each step requires a check.

actions.name: Request
actions.description: Ask for something. The target's attitude sets the DC — Helpful NPCs almost always oblige; Hostile ones almost never. GMs adjust based on the ask's reasonableness.

dcExamples.example: Make an Indifferent NPC Friendly, or request a minor favor from a Friendly contact
Gather Information about a recent public event
Persuade a Hostile noble to grant you an audience
Make an enemy general consider a ceasefire

intimidation.name: Intimidation
intimidation.description: You bend others to your will through threats or fear. Even untrained, you can Demoralize or Coerce.
commonUses: Demoralize an enemy mid-combat
Coerce information from a reluctant source
Recall Knowledge about criminal organizations or threats

actions.name: Demoralize
actions.description: Use fierce threats or presence to unsettle a foe. Intimidation vs. target's Will DC. Success inflicts Frightened 1 (Frightened 2 on a critical success). If you don't share a language, you take a -4 circumstance penalty.

actions.name: Coerce
actions.description: Force a creature to comply with a specific demand. Intimidation vs. Will DC. A critical failure makes them Unfriendly (or Hostile). A creature under duress will usually resume normal behavior at the next opportunity.

dcExamples.example: Demoralize a common guard
Coerce a prisoner into revealing a name
Demoralize a seasoned veteran or elite foe
Coerce a cultist to betray their god

medicine.name: Medicine
medicine.description: You can patch up wounds and help people recover from diseases and poisons. Even untrained, you can Administer First Aid.
commonUses: Administer First Aid to stabilize a dying ally or stop bleed
Treat Wounds to restore HP over time
Treat Disease or Treat Poison (requires Trained)
Recall Knowledge about diseases, injuries, anatomy, or medicine

actions.name: Administer First Aid
actions.description: Stop a dying ally from getting worse, or stop ongoing bleed damage. Requires you be adjacent. DC is set by the situation; success stabilizes the ally at 0 HP or ends bleed damage.

actions.name: Treat Wounds
actions.description: Restore Hit Points to an injured ally. Requires healer's tools. DC 15 heals 2d8 (plus bonuses by proficiency); crit success heals extra. Each ally can only benefit once per hour (10 minutes with Continual Recovery feat).

actions.name: Treat Disease
actions.description: Spend time tending to a diseased creature. On their next save vs. the disease, they gain a circumstance bonus based on your check outcome.

actions.name: Treat Poison
actions.description: Grant a poisoned creature a bonus to their next save against the poison. Requires you to be adjacent and have healer's tools.

dcExamples.example: Treat Wounds at standard DC (2d8 healing)
Treat Wounds for 2d8+10 (requires Expert, risky for Trained)
Treat a rare disease or exotic poison
Treat Wounds for 2d8+30 (requires Legendary)

nature.name: Nature
nature.description: You know about the natural world and primal magic. Even untrained, you can Recall Knowledge about wildlife, weather, and the wilderness.
commonUses: Recall Knowledge about animals, plants, weather, or primal magic
Command an Animal (Trained)
Identify primal spells and effects
Learn a primal spell (druids and similar casters)
Navigate natural environments

actions.name: Recall Knowledge
actions.description: Recall information about animals, natural environments, plants, weather, or primal magic.

actions.name: Command an Animal
actions.description: Direct an animal to perform a trick it knows. Nature vs. the animal's Will DC. A mount or animal companion has its own rules.

actions.name: Identify Magic
actions.description: Identify primal spells, magical plants, or natural phenomena caused by magic.

actions.name: Learn a Spell
actions.description: Study a primal spell to add it to your repertoire or list of prepared spells.

dcExamples.example: Recall Knowledge about a common animal or plant
Command a well-trained animal to perform a risky trick
Identify a rare primal phenomenon
Recall Knowledge about an ancient druidic circle

occultism.name: Occultism
occultism.description: You know about ancient philosophies, esoteric lore, obscure mysticism, and superstitions, plus occult magic. Even untrained, you can Recall Knowledge.
commonUses: Recall Knowledge about occult topics, aberrations, spirits, or planes
Identify occult spells and magical effects
Decipher cryptic, arcane, or occult writing
Learn occult spells

actions.name: Recall Knowledge
actions.description: Recall a fact about occult topics: spirits, aberrations, mind-affecting magic, esoteric lore, philosophy, or obscure cults.

actions.name: Identify Magic
actions.description: Identify occult spells, haunted phenomena, or objects of occult significance.

actions.name: Decipher Writing
actions.description: Translate obscure, cryptic, or occult writings. Works for secret ciphers, philosophical riddles, or fragmented tomes.

actions.name: Learn a Spell
actions.description: Study an occult spell to add it to your repertoire or prepared list.

dcExamples.example: Recall Knowledge about a common ghost story or fable
Identify an occult spell in progress
Decipher the diary of a mad cultist
Recall Knowledge about a secret occult society

performance.name: Performance
performance.description: You're skilled at entertaining others — acting, dancing, playing music, or telling stories. Even untrained, you can Perform for an audience.
commonUses: Perform for an audience or crowd
Earn Income as a traveling performer
Create a diversion through performance (instead of Deception)
Recall Knowledge about famous performers or art

actions.name: Perform
actions.description: Give a short performance to entertain or distract. The DC is set by the audience's sophistication and how impressive your display needs to be.

actions.name: Earn Income
actions.description: Work as a professional performer. Income scales with your level, proficiency, and the task DC.

dcExamples.example: Perform for a casual tavern crowd
Earn Income at a festival
Perform at a noble's court to sophisticated critics
Rival the greatest bards in the region

religion.name: Religion
religion.description: You know deities, religious traditions, and divine magic. Even untrained, you can Recall Knowledge about common faiths.
commonUses: Recall Knowledge about deities, religious practices, or divine creatures
Identify divine spells or holy artifacts
Learn a divine spell (clerics and similar)
Decipher religious writings or liturgical texts

actions.name: Recall Knowledge
actions.description: Recall information about deities, religious history, divine creatures (angels, demons, celestials), or faith traditions.

actions.name: Identify Magic
actions.description: Identify divine spells, holy symbols, or magical effects of divine origin.

actions.name: Decipher Writing
actions.description: Translate religious texts, prayers, or divine inscriptions.

actions.name: Learn a Spell
actions.description: Study a divine spell to add it to your prepared spell list or repertoire.

dcExamples.example: Recall Knowledge about a major deity's tenets
Identify a divine spell in progress
Decipher the sermon of a fallen prophet
Know the secret name of a specific angel or devil

society.name: Society
society.description: You understand the people and organizations of the mortal world. Even untrained, you can Recall Knowledge about history, laws, and customs.
commonUses: Recall Knowledge about history, governments, customs, or languages
Subsist in an urban environment
Create a Forgery (requires Trained)
Decipher codes, ciphers, or ancient writing

actions.name: Recall Knowledge
actions.description: Recall information about human civilizations, history, cultural customs, laws, politics, or languages.

actions.name: Subsist
actions.description: Find food and shelter in a city by begging, scavenging, or working odd jobs. DC depends on the city's wealth and your social standing.

actions.name: Create a Forgery
actions.description: Forge a document — letters of credit, signed edicts, legal papers. Society check sets the DC for anyone inspecting the forgery later.

actions.name: Decipher Writing
actions.description: Translate or decode ancient languages, mundane ciphers, or legal jargon.

dcExamples.example: Recall Knowledge about a recent political event
Forge a common noble's signature
Decipher an old imperial legal document
Recall a long-forgotten historical secret

stealth.name: Stealth
stealth.description: You're skilled at avoiding detection. You can Hide or Sneak even if untrained, but some effects require Trained or higher.
commonUses: Hide from enemies behind cover or concealment
Sneak past foes without being detected
Conceal an object on your person
Palm or pilfer small items without being seen

actions.name: Hide
actions.description: Attempt to become Hidden from observers. You must have cover or concealment. Stealth vs. each observer's Perception DC; success makes you Hidden, meaning attackers must succeed a DC 11 flat check to target you.

actions.name: Sneak
actions.description: Move up to your Speed while Hidden or Undetected. Stealth vs. Perception DCs of nearby observers. On a failure, you become Observed; a critical failure reveals your exact location.

actions.name: Conceal an Object
actions.description: Hide a small item on your person. Stealth vs. the searcher's Perception DC when later inspected. Concealing an obvious item like a longsword is impossible without special gear.

dcExamples.example: Hide from an unwary guard at night
Sneak past a watchful sentry
Conceal a dagger from a pat-down search
Stealth through the inner sanctum of an elite spymaster

survival.name: Survival
survival.description: You know how to live off the land. Even untrained, you can Subsist in the wilderness and Sense Direction.
commonUses: Subsist in the wilderness (find food and shelter)
Sense Direction when lost
Track a creature through terrain
Cover Tracks to avoid being tracked

actions.name: Sense Direction
actions.description: Determine cardinal directions and your approximate location. DC depends on the terrain and available landmarks.

actions.name: Subsist
actions.description: Forage, hunt, or find shelter in the wilderness. DC depends on the terrain's harshness. A party member can Subsist for multiple allies at a higher DC.

actions.name: Track
actions.description: Follow tracks or clues left by a creature. Repeated Survival checks as you move; the DC depends on how old and clear the trail is.

actions.name: Cover Tracks
actions.description: Move at half Speed while erasing your trail. Opposing Survival checks from pursuers. Useful when being hunted.

dcExamples.example: Subsist in mild forest terrain
Track a creature across soft ground half a day old
Sense Direction deep in an unfamiliar swamp
Track a skilled ranger who is actively Covering Tracks

thievery.name: Thievery
thievery.description: You're adept at nimble tasks like disabling traps, picking locks, and stealing. Even untrained, you can attempt simple Palm or Steal actions.
commonUses: Pick locks (requires Trained)
Disable Devices (traps, locks, mechanisms — requires Trained)
Steal an item from a person without being noticed
Palm a small object (sleight of hand)

actions.name: Palm an Object
actions.description: Pick up a small, light object without being detected. Thievery vs. the observer's Perception DC. Taking an object from a person requires the Steal action instead.

actions.name: Steal
actions.description: Take an item from another creature's person without them noticing. Only works on items not being actively held. Thievery vs. Perception DC.

actions.name: Disable a Device
actions.description: Use thieves' tools to disable a lock, trap, or mechanism. DC varies by complexity. A critical failure on a trap often triggers it.

actions.name: Pick a Lock
actions.description: Open a lock without the key, using thieves' tools. The lock's complexity determines the DC and the number of successes required.

dcExamples.example: Pick a simple lock or Steal from a distracted merchant
Disable a poorly built trap, or pick a decent city lock
Steal from an alert wizard mid-conversation
Disable a magical rune-warded lock on a vault
